blocks.md: APL is built on **arrays**. The simplest is a vector: just write numbers separated by spaces.
Arithmetic works on whole vectors at once, element by element. This is the heart of APL — no loops needed.
[[⍳]] (iota) generates the first `n` integers: `1 2 3 … n`. It is how you make a range.
`≢` (tally) counts how many items a vector has. `⍴` (rho, backtick-r) gives its shape as a vector.

blocks.prompt: Take the vector 1 2 3 4 and double every element, giving 2 4 6 8.
blocks.hint: a scalar applies to every element

blocks.prompt: Produce the squares of the first five integers: 1 4 9 16 25.
How many integers are in the range 1 to 50? Use a function that counts the items of ⍳50.
blocks.hint: ≢ tallies; it reads right to left so it counts ⍳50

blocks.prompt: Make the vector 11 12 13 14 15 by adding 10 to the first five integers.
